case_definitions.name: relative time needs date helper
case_definitions.task: For the last 30 minutes of Zymtrace data, prepare the time range I should use for generated project metrics API calls.

case_definitions.name: unknown project id uses search
case_definitions.task: Find the Zymtrace project named llama-inference before we inspect its metrics.

case_definitions.name: cpu hot functions
case_definitions.task: Show the hottest CPU functions from 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 to 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: hot containers use entities
case_definitions.task: Which containers consumed the most CPU between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00?

case_definitions.name: first trace discovery is meta only
case_definitions.task: Find the hottest stack traces for python workers between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00, but do not dump full stacks yet.

case_definitions.name: high level flamegraph
case_definitions.task: Give me a CPU flamegraph for the vllm executable from 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 to 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: discover metrics before query
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, what metric names and attributes are active between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00?

case_definitions.name: known metric query
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, query the known request_latency histogram for service api between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: generated optimization advice
case_definitions.task: Show already-generated Zymtrace optimization recommendations for CPU traces from 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 to 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: raw events are explicit
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, fetch a small raw event sample between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00 for debugging the event payload shape.

case_definitions.name: discover common filter fields
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, show the most common available filter field values across profiling events between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top pods endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank the hottest Kubernetes pods between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: default project metrics discovery skips search
case_definitions.task: For the default Zymtrace project, discover active GPU, CPU, model, service, token, and latency metric names between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: default project raw sample skips search
case_definitions.task: For the default Zymtrace project, fetch a small raw event sample between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00 to inspect payload shape.

case_definitions.name: cpu rank first containerized apps
case_definitions.task: Which containerized app should I optimize first by CPU cost from 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 to 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00?

case_definitions.name: gpu inference workflow starts with metrics
case_definitions.task: For the default Zymtrace project, start investigating why the vLLM inference workload has low GPU utilization between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00 by finding the relevant GPU, CPU, and framework metrics.

case_definitions.name: gpu call tree uses hot traces
case_definitions.task: Pull the GPU call tree for the vLLM workload from 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 to 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00, but only do first-pass discovery.

case_definitions.name: selected trace drilldown is bounded
case_definitions.task: Drill into hot trace prefix_hash 981237 and fetch exactly that full trace for the CPU profile from 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 to 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: full trace error recovers to discovery
case_definitions.task: The previous Zymtrace hot_traces call failed because meta_only=false was requested without a selected prefix_hash. Recover by doing first-pass CPU hot trace discovery for the default project between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: hot trace discovery is bounded
case_definitions.task: Show only the top 3 hot trace candidates as metadata for CPU between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00. Do not return full stacks yet.

case_definitions.name: resource fallback hot functions
case_definitions.task: In this tool-only Zymtrace matrix, choose the fallback call for the topfunctions MCP resource to rank hottest CPU functions between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project json flamegraph stays project scoped
case_definitions.task: For the default Zymtrace project, generate JSON flamegraph data for downstream processing between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project event aggregate counts
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, aggregate profiling event counts by kind between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: host metadata aggregation
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, aggregate host metadata between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00 to see OS, GPU, and architecture attributes.

case_definitions.name: project top architectures endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank the CPU architectures present in the profiling data between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top clusters endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank the Kubernetes clusters contributing the most profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top containers endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, use the generated project stats endpoint to rank containers by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top deployments endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank Kubernetes deployments by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top hosts endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank hosts by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top executables endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank main executable names by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top namespaces endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank Kubernetes namespaces by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top scripts endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank script names by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top threads endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank thread names by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

case_definitions.name: project top user tags endpoint
case_definitions.task: For project 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000, rank custom user tags by profiling activity between 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00.

description: Compare stock and tuned descriptions for the Zymtrace MCP profiling-analysis tools.

choice.rationale: The task uses a relative time range ('last 30 minutes') and requires Unix milliseconds for generated project metrics REST-style APIs. get_date_time provides the current time and machine-usable Unix milliseconds needed to compute the start/end boundaries before making any project metrics call.
The user names a non-default project (llama-inference) without its UUID and wants to find/switch to it before inspecting metrics; projects_search is the designated tool for locating a named project.

The user asks for the hottest CPU functions, which is the common profiling question best served by topfunctions. An explicit RFC3339 time range is provided, so no get_date_time call is needed. In this tool-only matrix, topfunctions is the correct fallback for the same-named resource.

The user asks for the top CPU-consuming containers over an explicit RFC3339 time range. This is a hot-entity profiling question, and topentities is the high-level fallback tool for hot executables/hosts/containers/pods. The entity is set to 'container' and profile_type to 'CPU'. Since the range is explicit, no get_date_time call is needed, and no non-default project is named.

results.case: first trace discovery is meta only

choice.rationale: The task is stack-trace/call-tree discovery, which maps to hot_traces. An explicit RFC3339 range is given, so no get_date_time is needed. The filter_text narrows to python workers, and meta_only enables discovery mode to avoid dumping full stacks yet.

The user requests a rendered CPU flamegraph with an explicit RFC3339 time range, so no get_date_time call is needed. In this tool-only matrix, the flamegraph tool is the fallback for a rendered flamegraph. It accepts profile_type, start_time, and end_time; the vllm executable scope isn't a supported parameter here.

The user asks to discover active metric names and attributes for a specific non-default project within an explicit absolute time range. Metrics discovery should use project_metrics_activity_aggr before project_metrics_query, and the given UUID is used directly as project_id (no projects_search needed since the UUID is provided). The range is explicit, so get_date_time is unnecessary.

The user names a specific known metric (request_latency histogram) with an explicit filter (service=api), an explicit project UUID, and an explicit time range. Since the metric is already known, discovery via project_metrics_activity_aggr is unnecessary, and project_metrics_query is the correct tool for querying a metric with custom filters. The explicit range and UUID mean get_date_time and projects_search are not needed.

The task explicitly asks for already-generated optimization recommendations for CPU traces, which matches the recommendations tool. An explicit absolute RFC3339 time range is provided, so get_date_time is unnecessary.

The user provides an explicit non-default project UUID, an explicit absolute time range (no relative time, so get_date_time is unnecessary), and explicitly asks for a small raw event sample to debug the event payload shape. This is a low-level raw event request within a defined project scope, matching project_events_raw. Times are converted to Unix milliseconds for the REST-style API.

The user names a non-default project UUID and asks for the most common available filter field values across profiling events with an explicit absolute time range. This is a low-level project-scoped aggregate over common filter fields, which is exactly what projects_events_aggregate_top_field_values provides.

Ranking the hottest Kubernetes pods is a hot-entity question, so the high-level topentities tool with entity=pod is preferred. Although a non-default project UUID is named, topentities has no project_id parameter, and the explicit RFC3339 range is provided so no get_date_time call is needed.

results.case: default project metrics discovery skips search

choice.rationale: The task is metric name discovery (GPU/CPU/model/service/token/latency metrics) for the default project with an explicit absolute range, so no get_date_time is needed. Per guidance, use project_metrics_activity_aggr before project_metrics_query for GPU/inference metrics discovery, scoped to the default project_id.

results.case: default project raw sample skips search

choice.rationale: The user has an explicit project scope (default project) and asks for a low-level raw event sample to inspect payload shape, which matches project_events_raw. An explicit time range is provided, so get_date_time is unnecessary, and the default project_id is used.

The user asks for the hottest containerized app by CPU cost within an explicit RFC3339 range, which is a hot-entity discovery question; topentities is the high-level fallback tool for hot executables/scripts/hosts/threads/containers/pods/apps, and the container entity matches 'containerized app'.

results.case: gpu inference workflow starts with metrics

choice.rationale: The task asks to discover relevant GPU/CPU/framework metrics for a vLLM inference workload with low GPU utilization. Per guidance, use project_metrics_activity_aggr before project_metrics_query, especially for GPU/inference metrics discovery. An explicit time range is given, so no get_date_time is needed, and the default project_id applies.

results.case: gpu call tree uses hot traces

choice.rationale: The task asks for the GPU call tree (stack-trace/call-tree discovery) for the vLLM workload, which maps to hot_traces. An explicit RFC3339 time range is provided, so no get_date_time is needed. 'First-pass discovery' indicates using meta_only discovery mode, and filter_text narrows to the vLLM workload.

The task is stack-trace/call-tree discovery drilling into a specific trace prefix_hash, which hot_traces handles via its prefix_hash parameter. An explicit RFC3339 range is given, so no get_date_time is needed, and this is a profiling-level (not raw project-scoped) request.

results.case: full trace error recovers to discovery

choice.rationale: First-pass hot trace discovery uses hot_traces in discovery mode (meta_only=true) so no prefix_hash is required, which recovers from the prior failure where meta_only=false was requested without a selected prefix_hash. The explicit RFC3339 range is provided, so get_date_time is unnecessary, and this is the tool-only fallback rather than reading the flamegraph/topfunctions resources.

The task asks for hot trace candidates as metadata only, not full stacks. hot_traces is the tool for stack-trace/call-tree discovery, and its meta_only discovery mode returns candidate metadata without full stacks. An explicit CPU profile type and absolute time range are given, so no get_date_time call is needed.

The task asks for the hottest CPU functions with an explicit absolute time range, so no get_date_time call is needed. In this tool-only matrix, the fallback for the topfunctions resource is the same-named topfunctions tool. profile_type is CPU and the RFC3339 range is provided directly.

results.case: project json flamegraph stays project scoped

choice.rationale: The task explicitly requests JSON flamegraph data for downstream processing scoped to the default project, with an explicit absolute time range (no relative/omitted range, so get_date_time is unnecessary). project_flamegraph_json is the project-scoped tool that generates JSON-format flamegraph output, distinct from the rendered flamegraph fallback tool. The default project_id is used since no other project was named.

The user has an explicit project scope (a named non-default UUID) and asks for a low-level aggregate of raw profiling events grouped by kind. This is exactly the raw project event aggregate use case, so projects_events_aggregate is the correct tool. An explicit time range is provided, so get_date_time is unnecessary.

The user provides an explicit non-default project UUID, an explicit time range, and asks for low-level host metadata aggregation (OS, GPU, architecture attributes). This directly matches projects_events_aggregate_host_metadata, a project-scoped raw aggregate endpoint. The range is explicit so get_date_time is unnecessary, and the UUID is given so projects_search is not needed.

The user names a specific non-default project UUID and asks to rank CPU architectures present in the profiling data over an explicit time range. This is a project-scoped low-level aggregate request, and projects_stats_aggregate_top_architectures directly returns top architectures. The range is fully specified, so no get_date_time call is needed, and the UUID is provided so projects_search is unnecessary.

The task asks to rank Kubernetes clusters by profiling activity within an explicit project scope (a non-default UUID) and an explicit absolute time range. This is a low-level project-scoped aggregate over clusters, which maps directly to projects_stats_aggregate_top_clusters. The explicit UTC range means get_date_time is unnecessary.

The user names a non-default project UUID and explicitly asks to use the generated project stats endpoint to rank containers by profiling activity. This maps directly to the low-level project-scoped stats tool for top containers, using the provided explicit time range.

Ranking Kubernetes deployments by profiling activity is a hot-entity question, so the high-level topentities tool is preferred with entity=deployment. An explicit time range in RFC3339 is provided, so get_date_time is unnecessary. Although a non-default project UUID is named, topentities is not project_id-scoped in its schema, and it is favored over the low-level projects_stats_aggregate_top_deployments fallback for common profiling questions.

Ranking hosts by profiling activity is a common profiling question best served by the high-level topentities tool with entity=host. Both the start and end times are explicitly provided in RFC3339 format, so no get_date_time call is needed. The topentities tool takes RFC3339 times and does not accept a project_id parameter, so the named non-default project is not passed here (and it is not a low-level raw/aggregate request that would require project-scoped stats tools).

The user names a specific non-default project UUID and explicitly asks to rank main executable NAMES by activity, which maps to the project-scoped raw stat endpoint for top main executables. The task provides an explicit absolute time range (convertible to Unix ms), so get_date_time is not needed. topentities could rank executables but the request is specifically for main executable names via the project's stat aggregation, and the project has a concrete UUID scope.

The user names a specific non-default project UUID and asks to rank Kubernetes namespaces by profiling activity. Both times are explicit absolute ranges, so no date/time resolution is needed. Namespace-specific ranking within a project scope maps directly to the project-scoped top_namespaces stats aggregate tool.

The user names a non-default project (with explicit UUID) and asks to rank script names by profiling activity over an explicit time range. This is a low-level project-scoped stat aggregation over script names, matching the dedicated projects_stats_aggregate_top_script_names endpoint. The range is explicit so get_date_time is unnecessary, and topentities is the profiling-abstraction fallback but the task explicitly scopes to a specific project's raw script-name stat aggregation.

The user names a non-default project UUID and explicitly requests ranking thread names by profiling activity within a fully-specified absolute time range. Both start and end times are provided, so get_date_time is unnecessary. The project-scoped stats aggregate for thread names directly answers this low-level ranking request, using the provided UUID rather than the default.

The user names a non-default project (with its UUID provided) and asks to rank custom user tags by profiling activity. This maps directly to the top user tags stats aggregation. An explicit absolute time range is given, so no get_date_time call is needed; times are converted to Unix milliseconds as required by the REST-style project APIs.

Preparing a 'last 30 minutes' time range for project metrics API calls requires a concrete, machine-usable current timestamp anchor. get_date_time provides the current local date/time and Unix milliseconds, which is the prerequisite for computing the start (now minus 30 minutes) and end (now) boundaries before any metrics query is constructed.

The task is to locate a Zymtrace project by name before inspecting metrics. projects_search is the only tool that finds projects, and it must run first to obtain the project_id needed by downstream metric tools.
The task explicitly asks for the hottest CPU functions, which is a rank-first CPU request best served by topfunctions over the given time window.

This is a rank-first CPU request asking which containers are hottest. Per the rules, CPU rank-first investigations start with topentities, using the container entity and CPU profile type over the specified window.

results.case: first trace discovery is meta only

choice.rationale: The task explicitly asks for hottest stack traces (hot-trace analysis) for python workers without dumping full stacks yet, so hot_traces is the right tool. Per rules, the first hot_traces call uses meta_only=true. This is CPU-only by default with a python entity filter.

The user explicitly asked for a rendered CPU flamegraph, and the rules state that when the user explicitly requests a flamegraph, choose flamegraph over hot_traces. Although 'vllm' is mentioned, the request is for a specific CPU flamegraph rendering, not a GPU/inference metrics investigation, so profile_type is CPU. The flamegraph tool schema has no entity filter field, so only profile_type and the time window are provided.

The task asks to discover which metric names and attributes are active in a time window for a specific project, which is exactly the metric activity discovery step served by project_metrics_activity_aggr.

The metric name (request_latency histogram) is already known, so per the GPU/metrics workflow we skip discovery via project_metrics_activity_aggr and directly query metric values with custom filters (service=api) over the specified time range using project_metrics_query.
The task explicitly asks for already-generated optimization recommendations for CPU traces over a defined time window, which is exactly the purpose of the recommendations tool.

The task explicitly asks for a small raw event sample to debug the event payload shape, which directly matches the project_events_raw purpose of fetching raw events. This is not a CPU/GPU ranking or hot-trace investigation, so profiling tools do not apply.

The task asks for the most common values across available filter fields for profiling events in a specific project and time window, which is precisely the purpose of projects_events_aggregate_top_field_values. This is not a rank-first CPU/GPU trace investigation, so entity/function/hot_traces tools do not apply.

This is a rank-first CPU/entity-only investigation asking which pods are hottest. Per the rules, rank-first CPU requests should start with topentities, using the pod entity to rank Kubernetes pods over the specified time window. CPU is the default for entity-only investigations.

results.case: default project metrics discovery skips search

choice.rationale: This is a GPU/inference-related request asking to discover active metric names. The GPU workflow mandates discovering matching GPU/framework/CPU metrics first with project_metrics_activity_aggr before querying values.

results.case: default project raw sample skips search

choice.rationale: The task asks to fetch a small raw event sample to inspect payload shape, which directly matches project_events_raw's purpose of fetching raw events. This is neither a ranking, hot-trace, flamegraph, nor metrics workflow, so profiling/ranking tools do not apply.

This is a rank-first CPU/biggest-ROI request scoped to containerized apps. Per the rules, CPU rank-first investigations start with topentities, and the containerized-app framing maps to the container entity. hot_traces drill-in comes only after selecting a user-owned entry.

results.case: gpu inference workflow starts with metrics

choice.rationale: This is a GPU/vLLM inference investigation, so the GPU workflow applies. The first step is to discover the relevant GPU, framework, and CPU metrics via project_metrics_activity_aggr before querying values or pulling hot_traces.

results.case: gpu call tree uses hot traces

choice.rationale: The task requests a GPU call tree (hot-trace analysis) for the vLLM workload and explicitly limits to first-pass discovery. Per rules, call-tree/hot-trace analysis prefers hot_traces, and first hot_traces calls must use meta_only=true. The vLLM filter is applied via filter_text.

The task asks to drill into a specific hot trace prefix_hash and fetch that exact full CPU trace. hot_traces is the correct tool for hot-trace analysis; since a prefix_hash is selected, this is a full trace fetch (limit=1 conceptually) rather than meta_only discovery.

results.case: full trace error recovers to discovery

choice.rationale: First-pass CPU hot trace discovery requires meta_only=true and no prefix_hash, recovering from the prior failure caused by requesting full traces without a selected prefix_hash.

The task requests hot trace candidates as metadata only (no full stacks), which maps to hot_traces with meta_only=true. Per rules, first hot_traces calls use meta_only=true and limit<=5; the top-3 metadata request satisfies this discovery mode.

The task is a rank-first CPU request to rank hottest CPU functions over a specific window. Per the rules, rank-first CPU requests start with topentities or topfunctions; here topfunctions is explicitly named as the resource to rank hottest CPU functions, so profile_type is CPU with the given time bounds.

results.case: project json flamegraph stays project scoped

choice.rationale: The task explicitly requests JSON flamegraph data for downstream processing rather than a rendered flamegraph view. project_flamegraph_json produces JSON-format flamegraph data, which matches the stated need better than the flamegraph tool that returns rendered/visual data.

The task asks to aggregate profiling event counts grouped by kind over a time window, which is a general event aggregation, not entity/function ranking or hot-trace analysis. projects_events_aggregate is the correct tool for aggregating project events; the other stats aggregators target specific dimensions (hosts, pods, etc.) rather than a generic kind grouping.

The task explicitly asks to aggregate host metadata to inspect OS, GPU, and architecture attributes for a specific project and time window, which is exactly the purpose of projects_events_aggregate_host_metadata.

The task explicitly asks to rank CPU architectures present in the profiling data over a time window. The dedicated tool for this is projects_stats_aggregate_top_architectures, which returns top architectures for the project. This is not a rank-first CPU hotspot/entity investigation (topentities/topfunctions), but a specific architecture aggregation.

The task asks to rank Kubernetes clusters by profiling activity for a specific project and time window. The dedicated tool for returning top clusters is projects_stats_aggregate_top_clusters, which directly matches this cluster-ranking request.

The task explicitly asks to rank containers by profiling activity for a specific project using the generated project stats endpoint, which maps directly to projects_stats_aggregate_top_containers with the given project UUID and time window.

This is a rank-first, entity-only (deployment) investigation with no GPU/accelerator signals, so it defaults to CPU and starts with topentities. The entity parameter is set to 'deployment' to rank Kubernetes deployments by profiling activity over the given window.

This is a rank-first CPU/entity investigation asking to rank hosts by profiling activity. Per the rules, CPU-only or entity-only investigations default to CPU and rank-first requests start with topentities, using the host entity.

This is a rank-first, entity-only CPU investigation (no GPU/accelerator signals), so it defaults to CPU and starts with topentities. The request ranks main executable names by profiling activity, which maps to the 'executable' entity.

This is a rank-first, entity-only CPU investigation asking to rank Kubernetes namespaces by profiling activity. Per the rules, entity-only/CPU-default ranking requests start with topentities, using the namespace entity for the given time window.

This is a rank-first, entity-only CPU investigation (no GPU/accelerator signals) asking to rank script names by profiling activity. Per the rules, rank-first CPU requests default to CPU and start with topentities, using the 'script' entity to rank script names.

The task explicitly asks to rank thread names by profiling activity for a specific project and time window. The dedicated projects_stats_aggregate_top_thread_names tool directly returns top thread names, matching the entity type requested.

The task asks to rank custom user tags by profiling activity for a specific project and time window. The dedicated tool projects_stats_aggregate_top_user_tags returns the top user tags, directly matching this ranking request.

The user gives a relative time window ('last 30 minutes') and needs it prepared for generated project metrics REST APIs, which require Unix millisecond timestamps. get_date_time provides current local time and Unix milliseconds to compute the range without hand-converting relative dates.

The user names a non-default project 'llama-inference' without providing its UUID and explicitly asks to find it before inspecting metrics. projects_search is the designated tool for finding/switching to a non-default named project, and it must resolve the project before any project-scoped metrics tools can be called.

The user asks for the hottest CPU functions over an explicit absolute RFC3339 time window, which maps directly to topfunctions with CPU profile_type. Timestamps are already provided, so no get_date_time call is needed. In this tool-only matrix, topfunctions is the correct fallback for the topfunctions resource.

The user wants a rank-first list of hottest containers by CPU. topentities is the high-level tool for ranking entities; entity=container matches the requested dimension, profile_type=CPU is default, and absolute RFC3339 timestamps are provided so no get_date_time call is needed. In this tool-only matrix, topentities is the fallback for the same-named resource.

results.case: first trace discovery is meta only

choice.rationale: The user asks for the hottest stack traces (call-tree discovery), which maps to hot_traces. Absolute RFC3339 timestamps are already provided, so get_date_time is unnecessary. Since they explicitly say not to dump full stacks yet, meta_only=true with a small limit is used for discovery, filtering by 'python' workers, defaulting to CPU.

The user explicitly asks for a rendered CPU flamegraph, not a call-tree drilldown, and provides absolute RFC3339 timestamps so no date_time lookup is needed. This is a tool-only matrix, so flamegraph is the correct fallback for the same-named resource. CPU is used as specified, and the named executable filter (vllm) is applied.

Absolute RFC3339 timestamps are already provided, so get_date_time is unnecessary.

Absolute RFC3339 timestamps are already provided, so no get_date_time is needed, and hot_traces is not a project-scoped REST tool requiring project_id.
